schema.appName: EPS Pension Calculator — Employee Pension Scheme
schema.appDescription: Calculate your monthly EPS pension at retirement. Based on pensionable salary and years of service under EPFO's Employee Pension Scheme 1995.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the EPS Pension Calculator. Smart defaults are pre-filled.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and find the best option.

faq.a: EPS pension formula: Monthly Pension = (Pensionable Salary × Pensionable Service) ÷ 70. Pensionable salary = average last 60 months' basic (capped at ₹15,000). Maximum pension (35 years, ₹15,000 salary) = (15,000 × 35) ÷ 70 = ₹7,500/month. Minimum pension is ₹1,000/month.

faq.q: What is the minimum service required for EPS pension?
faq.a: Minimum 10 years of eligible service is required for EPS pension. Less than 10 years: you can withdraw the EPS corpus (Scheme Certificate). Between 10–58 years: pension starts at 58. Early pension (age 50–58) possible with 10% reduction per year.

faq.q: Can I get higher pension under EPS?
faq.a: The Supreme Court 2022 judgement allowed employees earning above ₹15,000 to contribute to EPS on actual salary. This is the 'Higher Pension' option. Employees who opted by the May 2023 deadline can get significantly higher EPS pension — up to ₹15,000/month or more.

faq.q: Is the EPS Pension Calculator free?
faq.a: Yes, completely free on CalcPhi with no registration.
No. All calculations run in your browser. Your data is never stored.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones and tablets.

examples.heading: 20 years service, ₹15,000 pensionable salary
examples.text: EPS pension = (15,000 × 20) ÷ 70 = ₹4,286/month (₹51,432/year). This starts at age 58. Total EPS contribution over 20 years: approx ₹3,00,000. A ₹4,286/month pension on ₹3 lakh invested = very attractive long-term return.

examples.heading: Effect of 2 years extra service
examples.text: 30 years service at ₹15,000: pension = ₹6,429/month. 32 years: ₹6,857/month. Every extra year adds ₹214/month pension. Delaying EPF withdrawal to maximise EPS service years is financially smart.
